 {"id":"bd-1ez8","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase
- WezTerm release cadence: nightly builds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.398103664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:50:03.655977304Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ez8","depends_on_id":"bd-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:50:03.655935456Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ez8","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.407112375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zgb","title":"E2E: reliability hardening scenarios with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate resilience features end-to-end with detailed logging and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Execute fault injection scenarios that trigger circuit breaker, retry, and graceful degradation paths.
- Capture verbose logs (trace/debug) plus key metrics snapshots as artifacts.
- Verify user-facing output is stable and actionable during degraded mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scenarios pass locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, triggered fault labels, and observed policy outputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.619323361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826611461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"wa-37x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20fw","title":"Create WezTerm fork with wa feature flag and minimal integration points","description":"## Overview

Fork WezTerm and add the minimal code needed to support wa event delivery. All changes are behind \`#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]\`.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (WaEventSink trait design)

## Repository Setup

### 1. Fork WezTerm
\`\`\`bash
gh repo fork wez/wezterm --clone
cd wezterm
git checkout -b feature/wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Add Feature Flag
In Cargo.toml (workspace root):
\`\`\`toml
[workspace.features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

In relevant crates (mux/Cargo.toml, wezterm/Cargo.toml):
\`\`\`toml
[features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

## Modification Points

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
use wa_events::WaEventSink;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
static WA_EVENT_SINK: OnceCell<Arc<dyn WaEventSink>> = OnceCell::new();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {
    if let Some(sink) = WA_EVENT_SINK.get() {
        event(sink.as_ref());
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:
\`\`\`rust
fn process_pty_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
    // Existing WezTerm code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_output(self.pane_id, data);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit State Changes

In methods that update pane state:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_title(&mut self, title: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    self.emit_state_change();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn emit_state_change(&self) {
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_state_change(self.pane_id, &self.get_wa_state());
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 4. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit User-Var Changes

In the OSC 1337 handler:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_user_var(&mut self, name: String, value: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_user_var_changed(self.pane_id, &name, &value);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 5. wezterm/src/main.rs — Initialize Sink

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn init_wa_integration() {
    if let Ok(socket_path) = std::env::var("WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET") {
        match wa_socket_sink::connect(&socket_path) {
            Ok(sink) => mux::register_wa_event_sink(sink),
            Err(e) => log::warn!("Failed to connect to wa socket: {}", e),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 1. Build with Feature
\`\`\`bash
cargo build --features wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Default Build Unaffected
\`\`\`bash
cargo build  # Should work identically to upstream
\`\`\`

### 3. Integration Test
\`\`\`bash
# Start wa watch (which creates the socket)
wa watch &

# Start WezTerm with wa integration
WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET=/tmp/wa/events.sock ./target/debug/wezterm

# Verify events received
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WezTerm fork created with feature/wa-integration branch
- [ ] Feature flag added to workspace
- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths
- [ ] Event emission added to user-var handler
- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs
- [ ] Events actually received by wa (integration test)

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

## Files to Modify (in WezTerm fork)

- Cargo.toml (workspace) — add feature
- mux/Cargo.toml — add feature
- mux/src/lib.rs — event sink registration
- mux/src/pane.rs — event emission
- wezterm/Cargo.toml — add feature
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialization
- NEW: mux/src/wa_events.rs — trait + socket sink implementation

## References

- WezTerm mux: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Rust feature flags: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.103113431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:24.240940549Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20fw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:14.123064266Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20fw","depends_on_id":"bd-3gon","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:24.240909832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-21ml","title":"Benchmark WezTerm performance before/after STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removal","description":"## Overview

Measure WezTerm responsiveness before and after removing STATUS_UPDATE_LUA to quantify the performance improvement. This validates the optimization and provides data for the decision to pursue (or skip) Phase 2/3.

## Methodology

### 1. Baseline Measurement (Before)

With STATUS_UPDATE_LUA active:

\`\`\`bash
# Terminal responsiveness test
time for i in {1..1000}; do echo "line $i"; done

# Scrollback stress test
seq 1 10000 | while read n; do echo "Stress test line $n with some padding text to fill the line"; done

# Interactive typing latency (subjective)
# Type rapidly and observe lag
\`\`\`

### 2. Measurement (After)

Same tests with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed.

### 3. Metrics to Capture

| Metric | How to Measure |
|--------|----------------|
| Echo throughput | Lines/second in echo loop |
| Scroll smoothness | Subjective + frame timing |
| Input latency | Time from keypress to echo (harder to measure) |
| CPU usage | \`htop\` or \`perf\` during tests |
| WezTerm frame rate | If profiling tools available |

### 4. Test Scenarios

1. **Idle pane**: Just cursor blinking — update-status fires constantly
2. **Active output**: \`yes\` or \`seq\` flooding terminal
3. **Multiple panes**: 4+ panes, some active
4. **With wa watch running**: Baseline vs optimized
5. **Without wa watch**: Pure WezTerm baseline

## Tools

### CPU Profiling
\`\`\`bash
# Linux perf
perf record -g -p $(pgrep wezterm) -- sleep 10
perf report

# macOS Instruments
instruments -t "Time Profiler" -p $(pgrep wezterm) -D profile.trace
\`\`\`

### Frame Timing
WezTerm may have debug options for frame timing. Check config:
\`\`\`lua
config.debug_key_events = true
-- Other debug options
\`\`\`

### Memory
\`\`\`bash
# Watch WezTerm memory usage
while true; do ps -o rss= -p $(pgrep wezterm); sleep 1; done
\`\`\`

## Expected Results

| Scenario | Before (Lua) | After (No Lua) | Improvement |
|----------|--------------|----------------|-------------|
| Idle CPU | ~5-15% | ~1-3% | 3-5x |
| Echo throughput | X lines/s | Y lines/s | TBD |
| Typing latency | Perceptible lag | No lag | Subjective |

## Recording Results

Document results in:
1. This bead's comments (use \`br comments add\`)
2. PR description when merging
3. CHANGELOG.md under "Performance" section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Baseline measurements recorded (before)
- [ ] Post-optimization measurements recorded (after)
- [ ] Improvement quantified (percentage or ratio)
- [ ] Results documented
- [ ] User confirms subjective improvement
- [ ] Decision: proceed to Phase 2/3 or not?

## Dependencies

- Should be done: After bd-30gj (STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed)
- Before: bd-mwst (documentation) — to include benchmark results

## Files

- Create: benchmarks/lua_removal_results.md (or add to docs/)

## Notes

This is a **validation task**. If results don't show improvement, we need to investigate why (maybe the bottleneck is elsewhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:05.693319255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:15.720368063Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21ml","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:05.709998681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-21ml","depends_on_id":"bd-30gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:15.720326696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-27f3","title":"Add wa robot workflow status command","description":"# wa robot workflow status

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-status.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Query workflow_executions table for execution state.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id parameter
2. Query workflow_executions + workflow_step_log tables
3. Return status, current_step, step_logs, timestamps, error

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Returns execution status by ID
- Unit: Shows step progress accurately
- Unit: Error code stability (E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queries workflow_executions table
- [ ] Returns real-time step progress
- [ ] Includes step_logs array
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"bd-2kl3","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Optimize Remaining Lua (user-var hook) — If Still Needed","description":"## Overview

After Phase 1 (remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA), the only remaining Lua code is the user-var forwarding hook. This epic covers potential optimizations if that hook still causes performance issues.

**Priority P3**: This is a contingency. The user-var hook triggers infrequently (only when agents emit signals), so it's unlikely to be a bottleneck. Only pursue if performance testing shows issues.

## Remaining Lua Code

After Phase 1, wezterm.lua will contain only:

\`\`\`lua
-- Forward user-var events to wa daemon
wezterm.on('user-var-changed', function(window, pane, name, value)
  if name:match('^wa%-') then
    wezterm.background_child_process {
      'wa', 'event', '--from-uservar',
      '--pane', tostring(pane:pane_id()),
      '--name', name,
      '--value', value
    }
  end
end)
\`\`\`

## Why This Is Likely Fine

1. **Low frequency**: user-var-changed only fires when agents emit OSC 1337 sequences
2. **Filtered**: Only wa-prefixed vars trigger the handler
3. **Efficient check**: String prefix match is fast in Lua
4. **Non-blocking**: wezterm.background_child_process doesn't block the UI

## Potential Optimizations (If Needed)

### Option A: Remove Name Prefix Check in Lua

Move filtering to wa CLI:

**Before (filter in Lua)**:
\`\`\`lua
if name:match('^wa%-') then
  -- spawn process
end
\`\`\`

**After (filter in wa)**:
\`\`\`lua
wezterm.background_child_process {
  'wa', 'event', '--from-uservar',
  '--pane', tostring(pane:pane_id()),
  '--name', name,
  '--value', value
}
-- wa CLI ignores non-wa-prefixed vars
\`\`\`

**Trade-off**: More process spawns, but simpler Lua.

### Option B: Batching (Complex)

If many user-vars are emitted rapidly:
- Collect in Lua table
- Emit batch every N ms
- Reduces process spawns

**Trade-off**: Added complexity, latency.

### Option C: Replace with Native (Phase 2)

If using vendored WezTerm (Phase 2), user-var events go through native path anyway. No Lua needed.

## When to Pursue This Epic

1. Complete Phase 1
2. Benchmark: Is WezTerm still slow with just user-var hook?
3. Profile: Is user-var hook actually the bottleneck?
4. If yes to both: implement optimizations

## Success Criteria

- User-var forwarding works with acceptable performance
- No perceptible WezTerm slowdown during agent operation
- Latency for user-var events <100ms

## References

- USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA: crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs lines 33-47
- OSC 1337 user-vars: https://iterm2.com/documentation-escape-codes.html","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:51:22.219783156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:51:35.242788626Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kl3","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:51:35.242745746Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kl3","depends_on_id":"bd-2oby","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:51:22.233097688Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jfkk","title":"Audit and enhance ingest loop to provide all status metadata without Lua","description":"## Overview

Verify that wa's existing ingest/polling loop already provides all the metadata that STATUS_UPDATE_LUA was sending, and enhance it if needed. The goal is to confirm we lose nothing by removing the Lua hook.

## Current STATUS_UPDATE_LUA Payload

The Lua snippet sends this JSON to \`wa event --from-status\`:
\`\`\`json
{
  "schema_version": 0,
  "pane_id": 123,
  "domain": "local",
  "title": "zsh",
  "cursor": {"row": 10, "col": 5},
  "dimensions": {"rows": 24, "cols": 80},
  "is_alt_screen": false,
  "ts": 1706123456789
}
\`\`\`

## What Ingest Loop Already Provides

wa's ingest loop (crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs) polls \`wezterm cli list --format json\`:

\`\`\`json
[
  {
    "window_id": 0,
    "tab_id": 0,
    "pane_id": 0,
    "workspace": "default",
    "size": "80x24",        // ✅ dimensions available
    "title": "zsh",         // ✅ title available  
    "cwd": "/home/user",    // ✅ cwd available (bonus!)
    "domain": "local",      // ✅ domain available (new in WezTerm)
    "is_active": true       // ✅ active state available
  }
]
\`\`\`

## Gap Analysis

| Field | Lua Provides | CLI Provides | Action Needed |
|-------|-------------|--------------|---------------|
| pane_id | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| domain | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| title | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| dimensions | ✅ (rows/cols) | ✅ (80x24 string) | Parse "80x24" → (80, 24) |
| cursor position | ✅ | ❌ | See notes below |
| is_alt_screen | ✅ | ❌ | Handled by bd-2v4t (escape sequence detection) |
| cwd | ❌ | ✅ | Bonus - we get MORE data |

### Cursor Position

The cursor position was rarely used by wa. Options:
1. **Remove dependency**: Confirm no wa code actually needs cursor position
2. **On-demand query**: If ever needed, \`wezterm cli get-text --pane-id X\` includes cursor info
3. **Escape sequence parsing**: Could track cursor from CSI sequences (complex, probably overkill)

**Recommendation**: Audit wa codebase to confirm cursor position is unused, document as unavailable in polling mode.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Parse Dimensions String
\`\`\`rust
fn parse_dimensions(size: &str) -> Option<(u16, u16)> {
    let parts: Vec<&str> = size.split('x').collect();
    if parts.len() == 2 {
        let cols = parts[0].parse().ok()?;
        let rows = parts[1].parse().ok()?;
        Some((cols, rows))
    } else {
        None
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Domain Field Available
Check WezTerm version requirements — \`domain\` field was added in WezTerm 20230712+.
Add fallback for older versions (default to "local").

### 3. Audit Cursor Usage
Search codebase for any dependency on cursor position:
\`\`\`bash
rg "cursor" crates/wa-core/src/
\`\`\`

### 4. Update PaneInfo Struct
Ensure ingest.rs PaneInfo struct includes all fields:
\`\`\`rust
pub struct PaneInfo {
    pub pane_id: u64,
    pub title: String,
    pub domain: String,
    pub cwd: Option<String>,
    pub dimensions: (u16, u16),  // (cols, rows)
    pub is_active: bool,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,  // From ScreenStateTracker
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All STATUS_UPDATE_LUA fields available without Lua (except cursor if unused)
- [ ] Dimensions parsed from "WxH" string format
- [ ] Domain field handled with fallback for old WezTerm
- [ ] Cursor position usage audited and documented
- [ ] PaneInfo struct updated
- [ ] Unit tests for dimension parsing
- [ ] Integration test: ingest loop provides complete metadata

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2v4t (alt-screen detection) for is_alt_screen field

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs — PaneInfo struct and parsing
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm.rs — CLI output parsing

## References

- wezterm cli list: https://wezfurlong.org/wezterm/cli/cli/list.html
- Current ingest.rs: crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:26.071486794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:51.670537304Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:51.670460841Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:26.079402073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","depends_on_id":"bd-2v4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:35.987984256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Audit Complete - All Metadata Already Available

### Findings:

**1. Cursor Position** ✅
- `PaneInfo` struct already has `cursor_x: Option<u32>` and `cursor_y: Option<u32>`
- Populated by `wezterm cli list --format json`
- Test at wezterm.rs:1284-1285 confirms parsing works

**2. Dimensions** ✅
- `PaneInfo.size` has rows/cols (nested struct)
- Fallback to `PaneInfo.rows`/`PaneInfo.cols` (flat fields)
- `effective_rows()`/`effective_cols()` methods provide unified access

**3. Alt-Screen** ✅ (escape sequence detection ALREADY EXISTS)
- `PaneCursor.in_alt_screen` is updated during `capture_snapshot()`
- Uses `detect_alt_screen_changes()` at ingest.rs:1573 to find ESC[?1049h/l sequences
- When Lua removed: use `cursor.in_alt_screen` as canonical source

**4. Domain, Title, CWD** ✅
- `PaneInfo.domain_name`, `PaneInfo.title`, `PaneInfo.cwd` all populated from CLI
- `effective_domain()`, `effective_title()`, `parsed_cwd()` helper methods exist

### Action Needed:
None for data availability. The next task (bd-30gj) can safely remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA since all metadata is already available via CLI + escape sequence detection.

### Note on Two Alt-Screen Sources:
Currently IPC returns both:
- `alt_screen`: From PaneEntry.is_alt_screen (Lua IPC source - will become stale)
- `cursor_alt_screen`: From PaneCursor.in_alt_screen (escape sequence detection)

After Lua removal, consumers should use `cursor_alt_screen` as the authoritative source.","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mwst","title":"Document Lua removal: CHANGELOG, migration guide, architecture update","description":"## Overview

Update all documentation to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and the new polling-based architecture. This ensures users understand the change and can migrate smoothly.

## Documentation Updates Required

### 1. CHANGELOG.md

Add entry for the version that removes STATUS_UPDATE_LUA:

\`\`\`markdown
## [0.2.0] - 2026-XX-XX

### Changed

- **BREAKING**: Removed Lua-based status update hook (\`update-status\`)
  - Dramatically improves WezTerm performance by eliminating high-frequency Lua callbacks
  - Pane metadata now obtained via polling (\`wezterm cli list\`)
  - Alt-screen detection now via escape sequence parsing (more reliable)

### Removed

- \`wa event --from-status\` CLI command (internal, not public API)
- STATUS_UPDATE_LUA snippet from \`wa setup\` output

### Migration

If you previously ran \`wa setup\`, run it again to update your wezterm.lua:

\`\`\`bash
wa setup --wezterm
\`\`\`

This will automatically remove the deprecated Lua code from the WA-managed block.
\`\`\`

### 2. README.md / AGENTS.md

Update any references to status updates:

- Remove mentions of "real-time status updates"
- Update architecture diagram if it shows Lua→wa flow
- Add note about polling-based pane metadata

### 3. PLAN.md / PLAN_CODEX.md

Update the specification:

**Section to update:**
- §2.7 (Status update specification) — mark as deprecated/removed
- Appendix E.1 — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA documentation

**Add new section:**
- §X.X Polling-based pane metadata
- Explain how alt-screen detection works via escape sequences

### 4. Architecture Documentation

Create or update architecture docs explaining:

1. **Before (Lua-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   WezTerm update-status event (60Hz)
     → Lua handler (runs every event)
       → Rate limit check
         → JSON serialization
           → wezterm.background_child_process
             → wa event --from-status
               → IPC to watcher
   \`\`\`

2. **After (Polling-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   wa watcher ingest loop (configurable interval)
     → wezterm cli list --format json
       → Parse pane metadata
     → ScreenStateTracker.process_output()
       → Parse alt-screen escape sequences
   \`\`\`

### 5. Inline Code Documentation

Update doc comments in affected files:

- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs: Update module-level docs
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs: Document polling-based metadata
- crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs: Document escape sequence detection

### 6. Migration Guide

Create a dedicated migration section (can be in README or separate file):

\`\`\`markdown
## Migrating from wa 0.1.x to 0.2.x

### What Changed

In 0.2.0, we removed the Lua-based status update hook to dramatically improve WezTerm performance.

### Impact

- **No action required** for most users
- If you have custom integrations with \`wa event --from-status\`, see below

### Steps to Upgrade

1. **Update wa** to the latest version
2. **Re-run setup** to update your wezterm.lua:
   \`\`\`bash
   wa setup --wezterm
   \`\`\`
3. **Restart WezTerm** to pick up config changes

### Verifying the Migration

After upgrading, your wezterm.lua's WA-managed block should NOT contain:
- \`wezterm.on('update-status'\`
- \`wa_last_status_update\`
- \`WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS\`

It SHOULD still contain:
- \`wezterm.on('user-var-changed'\` (for agent signaling)

### Breaking Changes

- \`wa event --from-status\` command removed
- StatusUpdate IPC message type removed
- These were internal implementation details, not public APIs
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated with breaking change notice
- [ ] README.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] PLAN.md §2.7 marked as deprecated
- [ ] Architecture documentation updated
- [ ] Migration guide written
- [ ] Code doc comments updated
- [ ] All docs spell-checked and links verified

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-o2h4 (E2E tests passing, feature complete)

## Files to Modify/Create

- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md (if applicable)
- AGENTS.md (if applicable)
- PLAN.md
- PLAN_CODEX.md
- docs/migration-0.2.md (new, optional)
- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs (doc comments)
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs (doc comments)

## References

- Existing PLAN.md: project root
- WezTerm docs style: https://wezfurlong.org/wezterm/","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:14.119123581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:17.722765923Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-21ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:17.722732220Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:14.130330427Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-o2h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:23.903246697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-o2h4","title":"Update E2E tests: remove status_update scenario, add alt-screen detection test","description":"## Overview

Update the E2E test suite to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and addition of alt-screen detection via escape sequences.

## Current E2E Test State

The E2E test harness (scripts/e2e_test.sh) has a "status_update" scenario that:
1. Writes a minimal wezterm.lua with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
2. Spawns a pane
3. Sends a status update via \`wa event --from-status\`
4. Verifies the watcher receives it

This scenario will become invalid and must be removed.

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove status_update Scenario

In SCENARIO_REGISTRY (~line 340):
\`\`\`bash
# REMOVE this entry:
"status_update:Validate status update forwarding lane (wezterm.lua -> wa event -> watcher)"
\`\`\`

Remove the entire \`run_scenario_status_update()\` function (~lines 2550-2650).

### 2. Add alt-screen Detection Scenario

New scenario: "alt_screen_detection"

Purpose: Verify wa detects alt-screen transitions via escape sequence parsing (not Lua).

Test flow:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    # 1. Start wa watch
    # 2. Spawn a pane
    # 3. Send ESC[?1049h (enter alt-screen) to pane
    # 4. Verify wa detects alt-screen = true
    # 5. Send ESC[?1049l (leave alt-screen)
    # 6. Verify wa detects alt-screen = false
}
\`\`\`

Implementation sketch:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    local scenario_dir="$1"
    local wait_timeout="${TEST_WAIT_TIMEOUT:-30}"
    
    log_step "Starting wa watch..."
    wa watch --foreground > "$scenario_dir/wa_watch.log" 2>&1 &
    local wa_pid=$!
    sleep 2
    
    log_step "Spawning test pane..."
    local pane_id=$(wezterm cli spawn --cwd /tmp -- bash)
    sleep 1
    
    log_step "Verifying initial state (not alt-screen)..."
    local state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    local is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false initially, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen enter sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 h
    printf '\e[?1049h' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5  # Allow detection
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen detected..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "true" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=true after ESC[?1049h, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen leave sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 l
    printf '\e[?1049l' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen exited..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false after ESC[?1049l, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Alt-screen detection via escape sequences works!"
    cleanup_pane "$pane_id"
    kill "$wa_pid" 2>/dev/null
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Update uservar_forwarding Scenario

Ensure the uservar_forwarding scenario still works (it should, we're only removing status updates).

Verify it doesn't depend on status update functionality.

### 4. Add Regression Test: No Lua Status Hook

New scenario: "no_lua_status_hook"

Purpose: Verify wezterm.lua doesn't contain STATUS_UPDATE_LUA after setup.

\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_no_lua_status_hook() {
    # 1. Run wa setup
    # 2. Read wezterm.lua
    # 3. Verify it contains USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
    # 4. Verify it does NOT contain 'update-status'
    # 5. Verify it does NOT contain 'wa_last_status_update'
}
\`\`\`

## Test Fixtures

Create/update test fixtures:

### New: tests/e2e/alt_screen_enter.txt
\`\`\`
ESC[?1049h
\`\`\`

### New: tests/e2e/alt_screen_leave.txt
\`\`\`
ESC[?1049l
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] status_update scenario removed from SCENARIO_REGISTRY
- [ ] run_scenario_status_update() function removed
- [ ] alt_screen_detection scenario added and passing
- [ ] no_lua_status_hook scenario added and passing
- [ ] uservar_forwarding scenario still passes
- [ ] All other E2E tests unaffected
- [ ] CI passes

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2z6d (CLI handling removed)
- Depends on: bd-2v4t (alt-screen detection implemented)

## Files to Modify

- scripts/e2e_test.sh:
  - Remove status_update from SCENARIO_REGISTRY
  - Remove run_scenario_status_update()
  - Add alt_screen_detection scenario
  - Add no_lua_status_hook scenario

## References

- E2E test harness: scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Scenario registry: ~line 335
- Alt-screen sequences: ESC[?1049h/l","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:46:29.380681973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:41:20.415896064Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:46:29.396499877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2v4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:46:41.170218869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2z6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:46:39.100479976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qvbz","title":"Robot workflow subcommands: unit + E2E tests with detailed logging","description":"# Robot workflow subcommands testing

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing for the harmonized workflow subcommand structure:
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane>` (syntax change from bd-2kl8)
- `wa robot workflow list` (implementation in wa-35l)
- `wa robot workflow status <id>` (implementation in wa-7dd)
- `wa robot workflow abort <id>` (implementation in wa-55y)
- `wa robot events --unhandled` (alias from bd-2rwq)

## Unit Tests

### CLI Parsing Tests (Already Implemented)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_run_subcommand_parsing() {
    // Old syntax should fail
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Implicit run should be rejected");
    
    // New syntax should work
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "run", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_unhandled_flag_alias() {
    // Both flags should parse to same value
    let result1 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled"]);
    let result2 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled-only"]);
    assert_eq!(result1.unhandled_only, result2.unhandled_only);
}
```

### JSON Schema Validation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_list_json_schema() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-list.json");
    assert!(output["data"]["workflows"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_status_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-status.json");
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_abort_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-abort.json");
}
```

### Error Code Stability Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "nonexistent"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}
```

## E2E Tests

### Scenario: Full Workflow Lifecycle
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_lifecycle.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="$ARTIFACT_DIR/workflow_lifecycle.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow Lifecycle E2E ==="

# 1. List workflows
log "Listing workflows..."
LIST=$(wa robot workflow list)
log "List: $LIST"
echo "$LIST" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: list"; exit 1; }

# 2. Run a workflow (dry-run)
log "Starting workflow (dry-run)..."
RUN=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction 0 --dry-run || true)
log "Run: $RUN"

# 3. Check status (if execution ID available)
EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')
if [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "Checking status for $EXEC_ID..."
    STATUS=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID")
    log "Status: $STATUS"
fi

log "=== PASS: workflow_lifecycle ==="
```

### Scenario: Events Unhandled Flag
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_events_unhandled.sh
log "Testing --unhandled alias..."
O1=$(wa robot events --unhandled)
O2=$(wa robot events --unhandled-only)
echo "$O1" | jq -e '.ok' && echo "$O2" | jq -e '.ok' || exit 1
log "=== PASS: events_unhandled ==="
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every action
- Log raw JSON output for debugging
- Dump full output + stack trace on failure
- Summary: PASS/FAIL, elapsed_ms, key assertions

- wa-7dd: workflow status implementation
- wa-55y: workflow abort implementation
- wa-nu4.1.1: workflow engine core

 {"id":"bd-sx55","title":"Implement wa-side native event listener for vendored WezTerm events","description":"## Overview

Implement the wa-side Unix socket listener that receives events from vendored WezTerm's native integration. This replaces the Lua→CLI→IPC path with direct socket communication.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (Protocol design)
- Completed: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork emitting events)

## Implementation

### 1. Event Listener Module

Create \`crates/wa-core/src/native_events.rs\`:

\`\`\`rust
use std::path::PathBuf;
use tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream};
use tokio::sync::broadcast;

/// Events received from native WezTerm integration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum NativeEvent {
    PaneOutput {
        pane_id: u64,
        data: Vec<u8>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    StateChange {
        pane_id: u64,
        title: String,
        rows: u16,
        cols: u16,
        is_alt_screen: bool,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    UserVarChanged {
        pane_id: u64,
        name: String,
        value: String,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneCreated {
        pane_id: u64,
        domain: String,
        cwd: Option<String>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneDestroyed {
        pane_id: u64,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
}

/// Listener for native WezTerm events.
pub struct NativeEventListener {
    socket_path: PathBuf,
    listener: UnixListener,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
}

impl NativeEventListener {
    /// Create and bind the event listener socket.
    pub async fn bind(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Remove stale socket if exists
        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&socket_path);
        
        // Create parent directory
        if let Some(parent) = socket_path.parent() {
            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
        }
        
        let listener = UnixListener::bind(&socket_path)?;
        let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1024);
        
        Ok(Self {
            socket_path,
            listener,
            event_tx,
        })
    }
    
    /// Subscribe to events.
    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<NativeEvent> {
        self.event_tx.subscribe()
    }
    
    /// Run the event listener loop.
    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        loop {
            let (stream, _) = self.listener.accept().await?;
            let event_tx = self.event_tx.clone();
            
            tokio::spawn(async move {
                if let Err(e) = handle_connection(stream, event_tx).await {
                    tracing::warn!("Native event connection error: {}", e);
                }
            });
        }
    }
}

async fn handle_connection(
    stream: UnixStream,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
) -> Result<(), Error> {
    use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
    
    let reader = BufReader::new(stream);
    let mut lines = reader.lines();
    
    while let Some(line) = lines.next_line().await? {
        match parse_event(&line) {
            Ok(event) => {
                let _ = event_tx.send(event);  // Ignore if no subscribers
            }
            Err(e) => {
                tracing::debug!("Failed to parse native event: {}", e);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(())
}

fn parse_event(json: &str) -> Result<NativeEvent, Error> {
    let value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
    
    match value.get("type").and_then(|t| t.as_str()) {
        Some("pane_output") => Ok(NativeEvent::PaneOutput {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            data: base64::decode(value["data"].as_str().unwrap_or(""))?,
            timestamp: value["ts"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
        }),
        Some("state_change") => Ok(NativeEvent::StateChange {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            title: value["state"]["title"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            rows: value["state"]["rows"].as_u64().unwrap_or(24) as u16,
            cols: value["state"]["cols"].as_u64().unwrap_or(80) as u16,
            is_alt_screen: value["state"]["is_alt_screen"].as_bool().unwrap_or(false),
            timestamp: value["ts"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
        }),
        // ... other event types
        _ => Err(Error::UnknownEventType),
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Integration with Watcher

Modify \`crates/wa-core/src/watcher.rs\` or equivalent:

\`\`\`rust
impl Watcher {
    pub async fn run_with_native_events(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        let native_listener = NativeEventListener::bind(
            self.config.native_event_socket.clone()
        ).await?;
        
        let mut native_rx = native_listener.subscribe();
        
        // Spawn listener task
        let listener_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
            native_listener.run().await
        });
        
        loop {
            tokio::select! {
                // Existing poll loop
                _ = self.poll_interval.tick() => {
                    self.poll_panes().await?;
                }
                
                // Native events
                Ok(event) = native_rx.recv() => {
                    self.handle_native_event(event).await?;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    async fn handle_native_event(&mut self, event: NativeEvent) -> Result<()> {
        match event {
            NativeEvent::PaneOutput { pane_id, data, .. } => {
                // Feed to pattern detection
                self.pattern_engine.process(&data);
                // Feed to screen state tracker
                self.screen_tracker.process_output(pane_id, &data);
            }
            NativeEvent::StateChange { pane_id, is_alt_screen, .. } => {
                // Update pane state directly (no polling needed)
                self.pane_states.update_alt_screen(pane_id, is_alt_screen);
            }
            NativeEvent::UserVarChanged { pane_id, name, value, .. } => {
                // Same handling as IPC user-var events
                self.handle_user_var(pane_id, &name, &value).await?;
            }
            // ... other events
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Feature Flag

Gate native event support behind a feature:

\`\`\`toml
# crates/wa-core/Cargo.toml
[features]
native-wezterm = []
\`\`\`

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "native-wezterm")]
mod native_events;
\`\`\`

### 4. Configuration

Add config option:

\`\`\`toml
# wa.toml
[native]
enabled = true
socket_path = "/tmp/wa/events.sock"
\`\`\`

## Performance Comparison

| Metric | Lua Path | Native Path |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Event latency | ~50-100ms | ~1-5ms |
| Process spawns | 1 per event | 0 |
| CPU overhead | High (Lua) | Low (Rust) |
| Memory overhead | Lua VM | Minimal |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] NativeEventListener implemented
- [ ] All event types parsed correctly
- [ ] Integration with watcher loop
- [ ] Feature-gated code
- [ ] Configuration support
- [ ] Unit tests for event parsing
- [ ] Integration test with mock events
- [ ] Benchmark: latency comparison

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-3gon (protocol design)
- Depends on: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork emitting events)
